user_query:
I’m a Ukrainian worker living in Stockholm and I want to automate my daily search for evening/weekend part‑time jobs from official Swedish sources, and have personalized cover letters generated for the top matches.

Please build a small, runnable job that can be scheduled and also executed on demand. It must:

1) Read my local inputs from these files:
   - input/profile.yaml (my contact and profile info)
   - input/cover_letter_template.md (template with placeholders)
   - input/job_filters.yaml (keywords, allowed domains, schedule time)

2) Perform live Internet searches using a web search engine. For each allowed domain in input/job_filters.yaml (e.g., official Swedish sources like arbetsformedlingen.se, regionstockholm.se, stockholm.se), construct site-restricted queries that combine the include keywords and the location keywords from input/job_filters.yaml. Retrieve at least 20 unique result URLs across all allowed domains. Do not hardcode any URL. Save the raw search findings as JSON Lines to:
   - output/raw_search/YYYY-MM-DD/search_results.jsonl
   Each line must be a JSON object with: {"query", "source_domain", "title", "snippet", "url", "retrieval_timestamp"}.

3) Optionally fetch each result page to improve metadata, but it’s fine if you rely on titles/snippets when page parsing fails. From the result title/snippet and/or page content, extract best-effort fields: job_title, company (or "Unknown"), location_hint (must include Stockholm if present), and posted_date_hint (if a date is visible; otherwise blank).

4) Filter, sort, and rank the records as follows:
   - Filter: keep entries where (a) at least one include keyword appears AND at least one location keyword appears (both from input/job_filters.yaml) in the title/snippet or fetched page text; (b) none of the exclude keywords appear.
   - Rank primarily by the number of matched include keywords (descending), secondarily by posted_date_hint if available (newest first), and finally by the original search result order.
   - Write the filtered and ranked results to CSV at:
     output/listings/YYYY-MM-DD/filtered_listings.csv
     with columns: rank, job_title, company, source_domain, url, matched_keywords (semicolon-separated), location_hint, posted_date_hint, retrieval_timestamp.

5) Edit/rewrite the existing local cover letter template into tailored letters for the top N ranked results (N = top_n_letters in input/job_filters.yaml). Replace the template placeholders (e.g., {{job_title}}, {{company}}, {{url}}, {{source_domain}}, {{candidate_name}}, {{phone}}, {{email}}, {{city}}, {{availability}}, {{languages}}, {{skills}}, {{permit}}) using input/profile.yaml and the extracted listing fields. If company is unknown, address to "Hiring Manager" instead. Save one Markdown file per letter to:
   - output/letters/YYYY-MM-DD/cover_letter_<rank>.md

6) Scheduled task execution:
   - Create a runnable script (any language) and a simple shell wrapper at scripts/run_job.sh that executes the entire workflow and appends logs to output/logs. Produce a cron-compatible entry at:
     output/schedule/cron_entry.txt
     that runs scripts/run_job.sh daily at the time specified by daily_run_time_local in input/job_filters.yaml, using Europe/Stockholm time (include a comment noting the timezone). Do not install the cron job; just generate the entry file.
   - When you run the workflow once now, also write a log file to:
     output/logs/run_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.log
     summarizing counts: total URLs fetched, filtered count, and letters generated.

Requirements and notes:
- Use only the allowed source domains listed in input/job_filters.yaml. Do not add direct URLs in the code or the outputs, other than those discovered via search.
- The Internet search step is required (no local-only substitutes). The results will vary; correctness will be judged on the presence and structure of the outputs.
- All output paths above must be created. Ensure filtered_listings.csv has the exact columns listed and that the number of generated cover letters equals top_n_letters or the number of available filtered results, whichever is smaller.
- Keep the solution self-contained in this workspace. You may choose any programming language or libraries that can run locally. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
